source: Learning Ansible with Rocky - File Management: Moving files: copy, fetch, template, and get_url

questions.q: To copy a file FROM the Ansible server TO one or more hosts, which module?
questions.why: copy pushes a local file out to the target hosts.

questions.q: What does the fetch module do, relative to copy?
options: The opposite: copies a file FROM a remote host back TO the local server
Downloads a file from a URL
Edits an INI file in place
questions.why: fetch retrieves files from remote hosts to the control node (the reverse of copy).

questions.q: Which module generates a file on the target from a Jinja2 template?
questions.why: template renders a .j2 Jinja2 template to the destination file.

questions.q: Which templating system does the template module use?
questions.why: Ansible's template module uses the Jinja2 template system.

questions.q: What does the `validate` parameter (e.g. validate: '/usr/sbin/apachectl -t') do?
options: Runs a validation command on the generated file before putting it in place
Schedules a handler to restart the service
questions.why: validate runs a check command (if the service supports it, like apachectl -t) before the file is applied.

questions.q: To download a file from a website or FTP to the hosts, which module?
questions.why: get_url fetches a file over http/ftp onto the target.

questions.q: In get_url, what does providing a checksum achieve?
options: The file is not re-downloaded if it is already present and its checksum matches
It validates only the TLS certificate
It renames the file after download
questions.why: A matching checksum at the destination makes get_url skip the re-download, keeping it idempotent.

questions.q: Common to copy, template, and get_url, which parameters set ownership and permissions?
questions.why: owner/group/mode set the resulting file's ownership and permission bits.
